Traps#

Trap infrastructure#

class QHOT.lib.traps.QTrap.QTrap(r=(0.0, 0.0, 0.0), amplitude=1.0, phase=None, parent=None)[source]#

Bases: QObject

changed#

Emitted when any trap property changes.

property r: ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[float64]]#

Three-dimensional location of the trap [pixels]

property x: float#
property y: float#
property z: float#
property amplitude: float#

Relative amplitude of the trap field

property phase: float#

Relative phase of the trap field [radians]

leaves()[source]#

Yield this trap as its own sole leaf.

Provides a uniform interface with QTrapGroup.leaves() so that callers needing only leaf traps can call leaves() on any trap without checking its type.

Yields:

QTrap – This trap itself.

Return type:

Iterator[QTrap]

appearance()[source]#

Returns the visual properties of the trap

Override in subclasses to return keywords for pyqtgraph SpotItem (symbol, size, pen, brush, etc.)

Return type:

dict

isWithin(rect)[source]#

Returns True if the trap is within the rectangle

Return type:

bool

registerProperty(name, decimals=2, tooltip=False)[source]#
Return type:

None

setTrapProperty(name, value)[source]#
Return type:

None

property settings: dict[str, float]#

Current values of all registered properties.

Returns:

dict[str, float] – Mapping of property name to current value for every property registered via registerProperty.

to_dict()[source]#

Serialise this trap to a plain dict suitable for JSON export.

Return type:

dict

Returns:

dict – A dict with a 'type' key (the class name) and one key per registered property.

classmethod example()[source]#
Return type:

None

class QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapGroup.QTrapGroup(r=(0.0, 0.0, 0.0), amplitude=1.0, phase=None, parent=None)[source]#

Bases: QTrap

Trap composed of multiple traps or nested groups.

Subclass of QTrap. Iterating a QTrapGroup yields its direct children, which may themselves be groups. Use leaves() to iterate only the leaf traps at the bottom of the hierarchy.

traps#

Direct children of this group (may include nested QTrapGroups).

Type:

list[QTrap]

Signals#
-------
groupMoved#

Emitted when the group is translated. Carries the list of all leaf traps in the subtree and the translation delta as an np.ndarray. Emitted before the individual leaf changed signals so that observers can perform bulk invalidation.

Type:

QtCore.pyqtSignal(object, object)

groupMoved#

Emitted with (leaves, delta) when the group is translated.

leaves()[source]#

Recursively yield all leaf QTraps in this group.

Yields:

QTrap – Each leaf (non-group) trap in the subtree rooted here.

Return type:

Iterator[QTrap]

addTrap(traps)[source]#

Adds one or more traps or groups to this group.

Parameters:

traps (QTrap or list[QTrap]) – A single trap or group, or a list of traps/groups. Each item is added as a direct child of this group.

Return type:

None

removeTrap(trap)[source]#

Removes a trap from the group.

Return type:

None

property r: ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[float64]]#

Three-dimensional location of the trap [pixels]

to_dict()[source]#

Serialise this group and all its children to a plain dict.

Return type:

dict

Returns:

dict – A dict with 'type', the registered properties, and a 'children' list of recursively serialised child traps.

property traps: list[QTrap]#

Returns the list of traps in the group.

isWithin(rect)[source]#

Returns True if all traps are within the rectangle.

Return type:

bool

classmethod example()[source]#

Demonstrate group construction, translation, and removal.

Return type:

None

class QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapOverlay.QTrapOverlay(*args, size=16, parent=None, descriptions=((('left', 'shift'), 'addTrap'), (('left', 'ctrl|shift'), 'removeTrap'), (('left', 'alt|shift'), 'breakGroup')), **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: ScatterPlotItem

Graphical overlay for interacting with optical traps.

Renders each trap as a colored scatter-plot spot and dispatches mouse events to trap operations (add, remove, select, drag, group, break). Traps and trap groups are Qt children of the overlay; iterating over the overlay yields its top-level QTrap/QTrapGroup children.

Mouse gestures are configurable via the descriptions constructor argument, which maps (button, modifier) pairs to named handler methods. The default bindings are:

Gesture

Action

Shift + left click

Add trap

Ctrl + Shift + left click

Remove trap

Alt + Shift + left click

Break group

Left drag (no modifier)

Move group

Left drag (no target)

Rubber-band select / group

Scroll wheel

Adjust trap z

The overlay supports two event-delivery modes:

  • Standalone – embedded directly in a pyqtgraph.PlotWidget; Qt calls mousePressEvent / mouseMoveEvent / mouseReleaseEvent automatically.

  • Hosted – embedded in a QHOTScreen; the screen calls mousePress / mouseMove / mouseRelease / wheel explicitly with pre-transformed coordinates.

brush#

Fill brushes keyed by visual state.

Type:

dict[State, QBrush]

button#

Mouse-button name → Qt enum mapping.

Type:

dict[str, Qt.MouseButton]

modifier#

Modifier name → Qt enum mapping (supports |-separated combos).

Type:

dict[str, Qt.KeyboardModifier]

default#

Default gesture→handler bindings used when none are supplied.

Type:

Descriptions

Signals#
-------
trapAdded#

Emitted with the top-level trap or group after it is added.

Type:

QTrap

trapRemoved#

Emitted with the top-level trap or group after it is removed.

Type:

QTrap

trapAdded#

Emitted with the trap when a trap is added to the scene.

trapRemoved#

Emitted with the trap when a trap is removed from the scene.

class State(value)[source]#

Bases: Enum

Visual state of a trap spot, controlling its fill color.

STATIC = 0#
NORMAL = 1#
SELECTED = 2#
GROUPING = 3#
SPECIAL = 4#
brush: dict[State, QBrush] = {State.GROUPING: <PyQt6.QtGui.QBrush object>, State.NORMAL: <PyQt6.QtGui.QBrush object>, State.SELECTED: <PyQt6.QtGui.QBrush object>, State.SPECIAL: <PyQt6.QtGui.QBrush object>, State.STATIC: <PyQt6.QtGui.QBrush object>}#
button: dict[str, MouseButton] = {'left': <MouseButton.LeftButton: 1>, 'middle': <MouseButton.MiddleButton: 4>, 'right': <MouseButton.RightButton: 2>}#
modifier: dict[str, KeyboardModifier] = {'alt': <KeyboardModifier.AltModifier: 134217728>, 'ctrl': <KeyboardModifier.ControlModifier: 67108864>, 'meta': <KeyboardModifier.MetaModifier: 268435456>, 'none': <KeyboardModifier.NoModifier: 0>, 'shift': <KeyboardModifier.ShiftModifier: 33554432>}#
Signature#

alias of tuple[MouseButton, KeyboardModifier]

Handler#

alias of Callable[[QPointF], bool]

Mapping#

alias of tuple[tuple[MouseButton, KeyboardModifier], Callable[[QPointF], bool]]

Description#

alias of tuple[tuple[str, str], str]

Descriptions#

alias of tuple[tuple[tuple[str, str], str], …]

default: tuple[tuple[tuple[str, str], str], ...] = ((('left', 'shift'), 'addTrap'), (('left', 'ctrl|shift'), 'removeTrap'), (('left', 'alt|shift'), 'breakGroup'))#
addTrap(traps)[source]#

Register a trap or group with the overlay.

When called as a mouse handler with a QPointF, creates a new QTweezer at that position.

Parameters:

traps (QTrap or list[QTrap] or QPointF) – A single trap or group, a list of traps, or a position at which to create a new QTweezer.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – True on success.

removeTrap(trap)[source]#

Remove a trap from the overlay.

If the trap belongs to a group, the entire group is removed. When called as a mouse handler with a QPointF, removes the trap nearest to that position.

Parameters:

trap (QTrap or QPointF) – The trap to remove, or a position identifying the nearest trap.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – True if a trap was removed, False if no trap was found.

clearTraps()[source]#

Remove all traps from the overlay.

Return type:

None

static groupOf(trap)[source]#

Return the topmost parent of a trap, or the trap itself.

Parameters:

trap (QTrap) – A leaf trap or group.

Return type:

QTrap

Returns:

QTrap – The root of the group hierarchy containing trap.

startSelection(pos)[source]#

Begin a rubber-band selection anchored at pos.

Parameters:

pos (QPointF) – Anchor corner of the selection rectangle in item coordinates.

Return type:

None

growSelection(pos)[source]#

Extend the rubber-band to pos, highlighting enclosed traps.

Parameters:

pos (QPointF) – Current cursor position in item coordinates.

Return type:

None

endSelection()[source]#

Finish the rubber-band selection and group the enclosed traps.

Return type:

None

trapAt(pos)[source]#

Return the trap nearest to a position.

Parameters:

pos (QPointF) – Query position in item coordinates.

Return type:

QTrap | None

Returns:

QTrap or None – Nearest trap, or None if no trap is nearby.

trapsIn(rect)[source]#

Return all traps within a rectangle.

Parameters:

rect (QRectF) – Query rectangle in item coordinates.

Return type:

list[QTrap]

Returns:

list[QTrap] – Traps whose spots fall within rect, or [] if none.

groupAt(pos)[source]#

Return the topmost group containing the trap nearest to a position.

Parameters:

pos (QPointF) – Query position in item coordinates.

Return type:

QTrap | None

Returns:

QTrap or None – Root of the group hierarchy, or None if no trap is nearby.

breakGroup(pos)[source]#

Detach the clicked trap (or its subgroup) from its parent group.

Emits trapRemoved and trapAdded as needed to keep observers (e.g. QTrapWidget) consistent with the new structure.

Parameters:

pos (QPointF) – Click position in item coordinates.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – True if a trap was detached, False if no trap was found or the trap is not part of a group.

selectGroup(pos)[source]#

Select the trap group at pos for dragging.

Parameters:

pos (QPointF) – Click position in item coordinates.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – True if a group was selected, False if no trap is nearby.

mousePressEvent(event)[source]#

Handle press via QGraphicsItem dispatch (standalone / demo use).

Parameters:

event (QGraphicsSceneMouseEvent) – The mouse press event from Qt.

Return type:

None

mouseMoveEvent(event)[source]#

Handle move via QGraphicsItem dispatch (standalone / demo use).

Parameters:

event (QGraphicsSceneMouseEvent) – The mouse move event from Qt.

Return type:

None

mouseReleaseEvent(event)[source]#

Handle release via QGraphicsItem dispatch (standalone / demo use).

Parameters:

event (QGraphicsSceneMouseEvent) – The mouse release event from Qt.

Return type:

None

mousePress(event, pos)[source]#

Handle a press event forwarded by QHOTScreen.

Dispatches to the registered handler for the event’s button/modifier signature, falling back to selectGroup. Starts a rubber-band selection if no handler claims the event.

Parameters:

  • event (QtGui.QMouseEvent) – The original mouse event.

  • pos (QPointF) – Cursor position in item coordinates.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – Always True.

mouseMove(event, pos)[source]#

Handle a move event forwarded by QHOTScreen.

Drags the selected group when the left button is held, or grows the rubber-band selection if one is active. Ignores non-left-button moves.

Parameters:

  • event (QtGui.QMouseEvent) – The original mouse event.

  • pos (QPointF) – Cursor position in item coordinates.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – True if the event was handled, False otherwise.

mouseRelease(event)[source]#

Handle a release event forwarded by QHOTScreen.

Finalizes any active rubber-band selection and clears drag state.

Parameters:

event (QtGui.QMouseEvent) – The original mouse event.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – Always True.

wheel(event, pos)[source]#

Handle a wheel event forwarded by QHOTScreen.

Adjusts the z-coordinate of the nearest trap group by one step per notch of wheel rotation.

Parameters:

  • event (QtGui.QWheelEvent) – The original wheel event.

  • pos (QPointF) – Cursor position in item coordinates.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

bool – True if a group was found and moved, False otherwise.

save(path)[source]#

Save all traps to a JSON file.

Parameters:

path (str) – Destination file path.

Return type:

None

load(path)[source]#

Load traps from a JSON file, replacing the current configuration.

Parameters:

path (str) – Source file path produced by save().

Return type:

None

classmethod example()[source]#

Display an interactive trap overlay demo.

Opens a plot window with several individual traps and a grouped triple of traps. Supports adding, removing, grouping, and dragging traps interactively.

Return type:

None

class QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapWidget.QTrapPropertyEdit(name, value, *args, decimals=2, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QLineEdit

Single-property editor for one numeric trap attribute.

Displays a right-aligned, fixed-width line edit with a double validator. Emits propertyChanged(name, value) when the user commits a new value with Return or focus-loss.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Name of the trap property this editor controls.

  • value (float) – Initial value to display.

  • decimals (int) – Number of decimal places for display and validation. Default: 2.

propertyChanged#

Emitted with (name, value) when a trap property is edited.

classmethod fieldWidth()[source]#

Return the pixel width needed to display a typical value.

Computed from the application font metrics and cached per concrete class (not shared across subclasses).

Return type:

int

format(value)[source]#

Format a float with the configured decimal places.

Return type:

str

updateValue()[source]#

Read the current text, update the stored value, and emit signal.

No-op if the parsed value equals the currently stored value.

Return type:

None

property value: float#

Current numeric value displayed in the editor.

class QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapWidget.QTrapPropertyWidget(trap, *args, indent=0, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QWidget

Row of property editors for a single trap.

Creates one QTrapPropertyEdit per registered property and connects each to trap.setTrapProperty. Updates automatically when the trap emits changed. Call cleanup() before deletion to disconnect from the trap’s signals.

Parameters:

trap (QTrap) – The trap whose properties will be displayed and edited.

updateValues(trap)[source]#

Refresh all editors from the trap’s current property values.

Return type:

None

cleanup()[source]#

Disconnect from changed signal before deleting trap.

Return type:

None

class QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapWidget.QTrapWidget(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QFrame

Scrollable panel showing property editors for all active traps.

Maintains a QTrapPropertyWidget row for each registered trap. Traps are added via registerTrap and removed via unregisterTrap.

registerTrap(trap)[source]#

Add a property editor row for trap.

If trap is a QTrapGroup, also adds an indented row for each leaf trap beneath the group header row.

Return type:

None

unregisterTrap(trap)[source]#

Remove and destroy the property editor row for trap.

If trap is a QTrapGroup, also removes the indented leaf rows that were added by registerTrap.

Return type:

None

count()[source]#

Return the number of rows currently in the layout.

Return type:

int

classmethod example()[source]#

Display a QTrapWidget demo with several traps.

Return type:

None

class QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapMenu.QTrapMenu(*args, title='Add Trap', **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QMenu

Context menu for adding a trap of a chosen type to the overlay.

Populates itself from QHOT.traps.__all__ and emits trapRequested when the user picks an entry. Set pos before executing the menu so the emitted trap is positioned where the user clicked.

Parameters:

  • title (str) – Menu title shown in the menu bar. Defaults to 'Add Trap'.

  • *args, **kwargs – Forwarded to QMenu.

pos#

Screen-space position that will be assigned to the new trap. Defaults to the origin; set this before calling exec().

Type:

QtCore.QPointF

Signals#
-------
trapRequested#

Emitted with the configured pos and a freshly constructed trap instance when the user selects a trap type.

Type:

QtCore.pyqtSignal(QtCore.QPointF, QTrap)

trapRequested#

Emitted with (position, trap) when the user selects a trap type.

property pos: QPointF#

Position passed to the emitted trap.

QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapMenu.main()[source]#
Return type:

None

Concrete trap types#

class QHOT.traps.QTweezer.QTweezer(r=(0.0, 0.0, 0.0), amplitude=1.0, phase=None, parent=None)[source]#

Bases: QTrap

Single Gaussian optical tweezer.

Subclass of QTrap with no additional properties or structure. The trapping beam is a plain focused Gaussian; all behaviour is inherited directly from QTrap.

class QHOT.traps.QVortex.QVortex(*args, ell=10, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QTrap

Optical vortex trap.

Applies a helical phase ramp exp(i ell θ) to the trapping beam, producing a ring-shaped focus with a phase singularity at the centre.

Parameters:

  • ell (int) – Topological charge (winding number) of the vortex. Default: 0.

  • *args, **kwargs – Forwarded to QTrap.

ell#

Topological charge of the optical vortex.

Type:

int

structureChanged#

Emitted when the topological charge changes.

appearance()[source]#

Return the letter V as the scatter-plot symbol for this trap.

Return type:

dict

property ell: int#

Topological charge of the optical vortex.

structure(cgh)[source]#

Compute the helical phase structure exp(i ell θ).

Parameters:

cgh (CGH) – The hologram engine providing the angular coordinate array theta.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

Field – Complex phase mask of shape cgh.shape.

class QHOT.traps.QRingTrap.QRingTrap(*args, radius=10.0, ell=10.0, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QTrap

Ring trap.

Focuses light into a ring of radius radius using a Bessel-function amplitude profile combined with a helical phase ramp.

Parameters:

  • radius (float) – Radius of the ring [pixels]. Default: 10.

  • ell (float) – Topological charge of the ring trap. Default: 0.

  • *args, **kwargs – Forwarded to QTrap.

radius#

Radius of the ring trap [pixels].

Type:

float

ell#

Topological charge of the ring trap.

Type:

float

structureChanged#

Emitted when the topological charge or radius changes.

appearance()[source]#

Return the letter O as the scatter-plot symbol for this trap.

Return type:

dict

property radius: float#

Radius of the ring trap [pixels].

property ell: float#

Topological charge of the ring trap.

structure(cgh)[source]#

Compute the Bessel-function amplitude and helical phase structure.

Parameters:

cgh (CGH) – The hologram engine providing the radial coordinate array qr and angular coordinate array theta.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

Field – Complex structure mask of shape cgh.shape.

class QHOT.traps.QTrapArray.QTrapArray(*args, shape=(4, 4), separation=50.0, mask=None, fuzz=0.0, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QTrapGroup

Rectangular array of optical tweezers, with optional mask.

Subclass of QTrapGroup. Creates a uniform grid of QTweezer traps centered on the group’s own position. An optional boolean mask of shape (nx, ny) can suppress individual grid positions: True means the tweezer is present, False means it is absent.

The grid dimensions, spacing, and mask are all settable programmatically. Changing nx, ny, or shape resets the mask to None (full grid). Changing separation or mask preserves the shape.

Parameters:

  • shape (tuple[int, int]) – Number of grid positions along the (x, y) directions. Default: (4, 4).

  • separation (float) – Center-to-center spacing between adjacent positions [pixels]. Default: 50.

  • mask (array_like of bool, shape (nx, ny), optional) – Boolean mask selecting which grid positions are active. None (default) activates all positions.

  • *args, **kwargs – Forwarded to QTrapGroup.

shape#

Grid dimensions (nx, ny).

Type:

tuple[int, int]

nx#

Grid size along x. Registered property.

Type:

int

ny#

Grid size along y. Registered property.

Type:

int

separation#

Tweezer spacing [pixels]. Registered property.

Type:

float

fuzz#

Standard deviation of Gaussian random displacement applied to each trap position at population time [pixels]. Registered property. Default: 0. (exact grid positions).

Type:

float

mask#

Active-position mask, shape (nx, ny), or None for full grid.

Type:

np.ndarray or None

Signals#
-------
reshaping#

Emitted immediately before the existing tweezers are cleared.

Type:

()

reshaped#

Emitted after the new tweezers have been added.

Type:

()

reshaping#

Emitted when the array begins to reshape.

reshaped#

Emitted when the array has finished reshaping.

property shape: tuple[int, int]#

Grid dimensions (nx, ny).

property nx: int#

Number of grid positions along x.

property ny: int#

Number of grid positions along y.

property separation: float#

Center-to-center position spacing [pixels].

property fuzz: float#

Standard deviation of random trap displacement [pixels].

property mask: ndarray | None#

Boolean active-position mask of shape (nx, ny), or None.

to_dict()[source]#

Serialise this array’s parameters to a plain dict.

Overrides QTrapGroup.to_dict() to omit children, which are reconstructed automatically from shape, separation, fuzz, and mask when the object is re-created.

Return type:

dict

Returns:

dict – A dict with 'type', registered properties, and 'mask' (a nested list of bools, or None).

classmethod example()[source]#

Demonstrate creation and reshaping of a tweezer array.

Return type:

None

class QHOT.traps.QLetterArray.QLetterArray(*args, char='A', separation=40.0, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QTrapArray

Single dot-matrix character rendered as optical tweezers.

A specialisation of QTrapArray with a fixed 5-column × 7-row grid and a mask derived from the dot-matrix font. Each lit dot becomes one QTweezer; blank dots produce no trap.

Characters A-Z, a-z, 0-9 and space are supported. Upper- and lower-case letters use distinct glyphs. Unrecognised characters are rendered as spaces.

The nx and ny properties are fixed at 5 and 7 and are not exposed to QTrapWidget. Only separation is registered.

Inherits#

QFab.traps.QTrapArray

Parameters:

  • char (str) – Single character to render. Default: 'A'.

  • separation (float) – Center-to-center dot spacing [pixels]. Default: 40..

  • *args, **kwargs – Forwarded to QTrapArray.

char#

Current character. Setting this updates the mask and repopulates.

Type:

str

separation#

Dot spacing [pixels]. Registered property.

Type:

float

property char: str#

Single character rendered by this array.

classmethod example()[source]#

Demonstrate creation and character change.

Return type:

None

class QHOT.traps.QTextArray.QTextArray(*args, text='NYU', separation=40.0, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QTrapGroup

Dot-matrix text rendered as a pattern of optical tweezers.

Renders a string of characters using a 7-row × 5-column dot-matrix typeface. Each character occupies a fixed 5 × 7 cell rendered by a child QLetterArray. The text block is positioned so that the geometric centre of the character cells coincides with the group’s own position.

Characters A-Z, a-z, 0-9 and space are supported. Upper- and lower-case letters use distinct glyphs. Unrecognised characters are rendered as spaces.

Inherits#

QHOT.lib.traps.QTrapGroup

Parameters:

  • text (str) – String to render. Default: 'NYU'.

  • separation (float) – Center-to-center dot spacing [pixels]. Default: 40..

  • *args, **kwargs – Forwarded to QTrapGroup.

text#

Current text string. Setting this triggers repopulation.

Type:

str

separation#

Dot spacing [pixels]. Registered property.

Type:

float

Signals#
-------
reshaping#

Emitted before the existing letter arrays are cleared.

Type:

()

reshaped#

Emitted after the new letter arrays have been added.

Type:

()

reshaping#

Emitted when the text array begins to reshape.

reshaped#

Emitted when the text array has finished reshaping.

property text: str#

Text string rendered as tweezers.

property separation: float#

Center-to-center dot spacing [pixels].

classmethod example()[source]#

Demonstrate creation of a text trap array.

Return type:

None
